text:
Once upon a time in a quaint village surrounded by ancient forests, there lived a young fox named Willow. Willow was famous not just for her cunning nose but also her love for singing forest birds. One spring morning, a particular song caught Willow’s ear—a song unlike anything she’d ever heard before.

Curiosity piqued, she followed the melody deeper into the woods. What she discovered was a hidden meadow like no other. Rare butterflies flitted amongst petallike flowers, and golden rays filtered through the canopy. At the center was an old oak tree, its ring-expanded rings singing a language only wild birds could understand.

As Willow settled down amongst the branches, she listened intently. To her delight, the oak tree sang back—the most beautiful song she’d ever heard. It spoke tales of stars lost in cloud oceans, winds whispering through enchanted forests, and rivers singing through valleys.

Inspired by these songs, Willow decided to sing back. Her voice—a mix of owl dusk
